UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user of the u-bike platform who books rides or errands.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

name.description: The full name of the user.

favoriteRiderIds.description: References to Riders marked as favorite by the user. (Relationship: User N:N Rider)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was last updated.

RiderProfile.title: RiderProfile
RiderProfile.description: Represents a rider providing services on the u-bike platform, including their details, availability, and service types.

id.description: Unique identifier for the RiderProfile entity.

externalAuthId.description: Reference ID from an external authentication system for the rider. (Note: Passwords are not stored here).

name.description: The full name of the rider.

phoneNumber.description: The rider's contact phone number, used for authentication and contact.

bikeType.description: The type of bike the rider uses (e.g., 'Normal', 'Electric').

bikeDetails.description: Specific details about the rider's bike, such as model and license plate.

averageRating.description: The average rating received by the rider from completed trips. (Derived value).

isAvailable.description: Indicates whether the rider is currently available to accept new requests.

riderServiceTypes.description: An array of services the rider is configured to provide (e.g., 'PassengerRides', 'Errands', 'Both').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the rider profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the rider profile was last updated.

RideRequest.title: RideRequest
RideRequest.description: Represents a user's request for a motorbike ride to transport a passenger.

id.description: Unique identifier for the RideRequest entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who made the request. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N RideRequest)

riderId.description: Reference to the RiderProfile assigned to this request. (Relationship: RiderProfile 1:N RideRequest)

pickupLocation.description: The starting location for the ride.

destination.description: The final destination for the ride.

rideType.description: The type of bike requested for the ride ('Normal' or 'Electric').

estimatedPrice.description: The estimated price for the ride at the time of booking.

actualPrice.description: The final actual price charged for the completed ride.

distanceKm.description: The calculated distance of the ride in kilometers.

status.description: The current status of the ride request (e.g., 'Pending', 'Searching', 'Accepted', 'InProgress', 'Completed', 'Cancelled').

requestedAt.description: Timestamp when the ride request was made.

acceptedAt.description: Timestamp when a rider accepted the request.

startedAt.description: Timestamp when the ride officially started.

completedAt.description: Timestamp when the ride was completed.

passengerName.description: Name of the passenger if the ride is booked for someone else. Optional.

passengerPhoneNumber.description: Phone number of the passenger if the ride is booked for someone else. Optional.

ErrandRequest.title: ErrandRequest
ErrandRequest.description: Represents a user's request for a motorbike rider to deliver or pick up items.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ErrandRequest entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who made the errand request. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N ErrandRequest)

riderId.description: Reference to the RiderProfile assigned to this errand. (Relationship: RiderProfile 1:N ErrandRequest)

pickupLocation.description: The location where the item needs to be picked up.

deliveryLocation.description: The location where the item needs to be delivered.

itemDescription.description: A description of the item(s) to be transported.

itemSize.description: The estimated size of the item(s) ('Small', 'Medium', 'Large').

estimatedPrice.description: The estimated price for the errand at the time of booking.

actualPrice.description: The final actual price charged for the completed errand.

distanceKm.description: The calculated distance of the errand in kilometers.

status.description: The current status of the errand request (e.g., 'Pending', 'Searching', 'Accepted', 'InProgress', 'Completed', 'Cancelled').

requestedAt.description: Timestamp when the errand request was made.

acceptedAt.description: Timestamp when a rider accepted the errand request.

startedAt.description: Timestamp when the errand officially started (e.g., pickup initiated).

completedAt.description: Timestamp when the errand was completed (e.g., item delivered).

PaymentTransaction.title: PaymentTransaction
PaymentTransaction.description: Records details of a financial transaction related to a ride or errand on the platform.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PaymentTransaction entity.

rideRequestId.description: Reference to the RideRequest if this transaction is for a passenger ride. Optional. (Relationship: RideRequest 1:N PaymentTransaction)

errandRequestId.description: Reference to the ErrandRequest if this transaction is for an errand. Optional. (Relationship: ErrandRequest 1:N PaymentTransaction)

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile involved in this transaction (payer). (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N PaymentTransaction)

riderId.description: Reference to the RiderProfile involved in this transaction (receiver for payout/commission). Optional. (Relationship: RiderProfile 1:N PaymentTransaction)

amount.description: The monetary amount of the transaction.

currency.description: The currency code of the transaction (e.g., 'KSH', 'USD').

paymentMethod.description: The method used for the payment (e.g., 'M-Pesa', 'Credit Card').

externalTransactionId.description: An identifier from the external payment provider (e.g., M-Pesa transaction ID).

RideFeedback.title: RideFeedback
RideFeedback.description: Stores feedback provided by a user after completing a ride or errand.

id.description: Unique identifier for the RideFeedback entity.

rideRequestId.description: Reference to the RideRequest this feedback pertains to. Optional if for an errand. (Relationship: RideRequest 1:N RideFeedback)

errandRequestId.description: Reference to the ErrandRequest this feedback pertains to. Optional if for a ride. (Relationship: ErrandRequest 1:N RideFeedback)

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who provided the feedback. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N RideFeedback)

riderId.description: Reference to the RiderProfile who received the feedback. (Relationship: RiderProfile 1:N RideFeedback)

rating.description: The star rating given by the user (typically 1-5).

comment.description: Optional textual comments provided by the user.

feedbackDate.description: Timestamp when the feedback was submitted.

RiderWallet.title: RiderWallet
RiderWallet.description: Manages a rider's financial balance, total earnings, and summary of completed trips.

id.description: Unique identifier for the RiderWallet entity.

riderId.description: Reference to the RiderProfile associated with this wallet. (Relationship: RiderProfile 1:1 RiderWallet)

currentBalance.description: The current available balance in the rider's wallet.

totalEarnings.description: The cumulative total earnings of the rider from all completed trips/errands.

tripsCompleted.description: The total number of rides and errands completed by the rider.

lastWithdrawalDate.description: Timestamp of the rider's last withdrawal from the wallet.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the rider wallet was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the rider wallet was last updated.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. The document ID `{userId}` MUST match the Firebase Authentication UID of the user. This path uses path-based ownership for secure user access.

Stores individual rider profiles. The document ID `{riderId}` MUST match the Firebase Authentication UID of the rider. This path uses path-based ownership for secure rider access. The `externalAuthId` field within the document also redundantly stores the rider's UID for rule clarity.

params.name: riderId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the rider.

definition.description: Contains all ride requests made by users. Includes denormalized `userId` and `riderId` fields for authorization independence, allowing both the user who made the request and the assigned rider to access their respective ride details without hierarchical lookups.

params.name: rideRequestId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific ride request.

definition.description: Contains all errand requests made by users. This new collection includes denormalized `userId` and `riderId` fields for authorization independence, enabling both the requesting user and the assigned rider to securely access errand details.

params.name: errandRequestId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific errand request.

definition.description: Records all financial transactions. Includes denormalized `userId` and optionally `riderId` fields for authorization independence, allowing the user (payer) and associated rider to view relevant transactions without hierarchical lookups.

params.name: transactionId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific payment transaction.

definition.description: Stores all ride and errand feedback. Includes denormalized `userId` (who gave feedback) and `riderId` (who received feedback) fields for authorization independence, allowing both parties to access their respective feedback records.

params.name: feedbackId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific feedback entry.

definition.description: Manages individual rider financial wallets. The document ID `{riderId}` MUST match the Firebase Authentication UID of the rider, ensuring direct path-based ownership. The `riderId` field within the document also redundantly stores the rider's UID.

params.name: riderId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the rider to whom the wallet belongs.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to be secure, scalable, and debuggable by strictly adhering to the core design principles, especially Authorization Independence and Structural Segregation.

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization:**
For all transactional and collaborative entities such as `RideRequest`, `ErrandRequest`, `PaymentTransaction`, and `RideFeedback`, the `userId` and `riderId` (if applicable) are explicitly stored within each document. This denormalization is critical as it eliminates the need for expensive and insecure `get()` calls in Firestore security rules to check parent documents for authorization context. For example, to check if a user can access a `RideRequest`, rules only need to verify `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId` or `request.auth.uid == resource.data.riderId`. This design ensures atomic operations (transactions/batches) remain robust and makes security rules straightforward and highly performant.

For `UserProfile`, `RiderProfile`, and `RiderWallet`, the document ID itself corresponds to `request.auth.uid` (or `externalAuthId` for `RiderProfile`), directly establishing ownership and eliminating any authorization dependencies.

**Structural Segregation and QAPs (Queries as Authorization Primitives):**
Each top-level collection is designed to hold documents with a homogeneous security posture, simplifying security rules. For instance, all documents in `/ride_requests` have the same access requirements, governed by the `userId` and `riderId` fields within them. This segregation allows for easy-to-understand and maintain security rules.

The structure facilitates robust QAPs:
*   **User-specific lists:** Users can efficiently query their own ride requests (`/ride_requests?userId=<uid>`), errand requests (`/errand_requests?userId=<uid>`), payment transactions (`/payment_transactions?userId=<uid>`), and feedback (`/ride_feedback?userId=<uid>`) using simple `where` clauses on the denormalized `userId` field.
*   **Rider-specific lists:** Similarly, riders can query their assigned ride requests (`/ride_requests?riderId=<uid>`), errand requests (`/errand_requests?riderId=<uid>`), payment transactions (`/payment_transactions?riderId=<uid>`), and received feedback (`/ride_feedback?riderId=<uid>`) using `where` clauses on the denormalized `riderId` field.
*   **Direct access:** User profiles (`/user_profiles/{userId}`), rider profiles (`/rider_profiles/{riderId}`), and rider wallets (`/rider_wallets/{riderId}`) are directly accessible by their respective owners using `request.auth.uid` in the path, making `get()` operations secure and fast.

This design ensures that client-side queries can be directly translated into secure server-side authorization checks without complex rule logic, thus minimizing the risk of data leakage and improving debugging efficiency.
